faqs.question: What is the difference between pipe bursting and pipe lining?
faqs.answer: Pipe bursting replaces the old pipe by pulling a new one through while breaking apart the existing pipe. Pipe lining (CIPP) inserts a resin-coated liner into the existing pipe that hardens to form a new pipe within the old one. Both are trenchless methods.

faqs.question: When is pipe bursting recommended over pipe lining?
faqs.answer: Pipe bursting is typically recommended when the existing pipe is severely collapsed, back-bellied, or too damaged for lining. It's also preferred when you need to upsize the pipe diameter. Our technicians will recommend the best option after a camera inspection.

faqs.question: How long does the pipe bursting or lining process take?
faqs.answer: Most residential pipe bursting or lining jobs can be completed in one day. Larger or more complex projects may take 2–3 days. We'll provide a clear timeline during your consultation.
